name: Bill Clinton
description: President of the United States from 1993 to 2001 (born 1946)
abstract: William Jefferson  Clinton (né Blythe III; born August 19, 1946) is an American former politician and lawyer  who served as the 42nd president of the United States from 1993 to 2001. A member of the Democratic Party, he previously served as the attorney general of Arkansas from 1977 to 1979 and as the governor of Arkansas from 1979 to 19…

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Having met him then, and I have a picture of that particular thing at the White House where Bill Clinton is pointing at my saxophone and he is talking to me... I was at the table with Bill and Hillary
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Clinton is mentioned by the interviewer in a political context; the interviewee (George Russell) does not mention him or interact with him in the transcript.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: He came up here with Dollie and went into one of the rehearsal halls... when the President was finally introduced
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: I had a chance to work with him once at the White House... Bill Clinton played saxophone on “All Blues.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Cecil Taylor played in the White House, I think it was under Bill Clinton, because he had a saxophone, you know, Bill Clinton had a saxophone. In fact I got his CD, Bill Clinton’s CD when he played saxophone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Now, the current President – I’ve been down there several times for Clinton. He’s really into it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
